title: rubus × aspratilis
intro: Rubus × aspratilis, a hybrid plant species, is a cross between Rubus idaeus (wild raspberry) and Rubus fruticosus (blackberry), known for its thorny canes and aggregate fruits. The medicinal properties of Rubus × aspratilis include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high content of ellagic acid, anthocyanins, and other polyphenols. The health benefits of consuming Rubus × aspratilis include its ability to support cardiovascular health, boost the immune system, and protect against certain types of cancer, as well as its potential to improve cognitive function and reduce inflammation. Rubus × aspratilis can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be consumed as a tea, added to food or beverages, or used topically for skin and wound care.
